audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Elihu's entrance: friends stop answering, Elihu's anger at Job and the friends, youth/age wisdom tension, spirit/breath as source of understanding, and his compulsion to speak. Marked as Elihu's speech frame, not final theology.

verses.restored: These three men stopped answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
Then the anger of Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite, from the clan of Ram, burned. Against Job his anger burned because he justified himself rather than the Divine.
Against his three friends his anger also burned, because they had found no answer and yet had declared Job guilty.
Elihu had waited to speak to Job because they were older than he.
When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of the three men, his anger burned.
Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said: I am young in days, and you are aged; therefore I held back and feared to declare my knowledge to you.
I said, Days should speak, and many years should make wisdom known.
But truly it is a spirit in a mortal, the breath of Shaddai, that gives them understanding.
It is not the great who are wise, nor do the aged understand justice.
Therefore I say, Listen to me; I too will declare my knowledge.
Look, I waited for your words; I listened for your understandings while you searched out speeches.
I gave attention to you, and look, none of you refuted Job or answered his words.
Do not say, We have found wisdom; the Divine will defeat him, not a man.
He has not arranged words against me, and with your sayings I will not answer him.
They are dismayed; they answer no more; words have departed from them.
Shall I wait because they do not speak, because they stand still and answer no more?
I too will answer my share; I too will declare my knowledge.
For I am full of words; the spirit within me presses me.
Look, my belly is like wine not opened, like new wineskins ready to burst.
I will speak, that there may be relief for me; I will open my lips and answer.
Please let me not show partiality to anyone, nor flatter any human.
For I do not know how to flatter; soon my Maker would take me away.
